refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use, ensuring data acquisition, processing, and dissemination meet the latency target.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into sub-functions addressing data acquisition, processing, and dissemination, which are the key contributors to latency.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data capture to worksite model update availability; verify each sub-function meets its allocated latency budget.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within required time constraints.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model with acquired data efficiently to meet latency targets.
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules and stakeholders within latency requirements.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with prioritized network protocols.
DP2: Implement incremental model update algorithms optimized for low-latency processing.
DP3: Employ event-driven dissemination mechanisms over reliable network links.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address acquisition, processing, and dissemination respectively.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry acquisition and processing due to data freshness requirements.
Dependency of dissemination timing on processing completion.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via well-defined interfaces and asynchronous event handling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link reliability and bandwidth are sufficient to support telemetry and dissemination.
Risk of processing delays due to unexpected data volume spikes.
Assumes telemetry data sources provide timely and accurate data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor real-world data rates and processing times to adjust DP parameters or add buffering if needed.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry acquisition, processing, and dissemination of worksite model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Used for data transmission between NGHE Solution and other modules including Teleoperation and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Teleoperation and other consumers.

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary module containing core update logic and data processing.
Provides telemetry data but does not process or disseminate updates.
Consumes updated worksite model but does not participate in update generation.
Receives safety alerts but not involved in worksite model update.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update logic in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and supports latency requirements.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded.
Interface latency on Network Link could impact update timeliness.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing or modularizing update processing within NGHE Solution if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Centralization in NGHE Solution may increase diffusion risk if not properly modularized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the best architectural boundary for update logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end measuring latency across interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to NGHE Solution with well-defined interfaces to other modules.

recommended_revisions: Monitor real-time performance to validate latency assumptions.
Evaluate potential need for sub-module decomposition within NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment are consistent, feasible, and meet latency requirements with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within required time constraints.
FR2: Process and update the worksite model with acquired data efficiently to meet latency targets.
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules and stakeholders within latency requirements.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; next steps involve deeper decomposition of sub-FRs.

assumptions: Network link reliability and bandwidth are sufficient.
Telemetry data sources provide timely and accurate data.
open_risks: Processing delays due to data volume spikes.
Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements from prototype or simulation.
Network performance statistics under operational conditions.
